binding_constraint_top1: Foundational planning-and-governance failure — the authority is operating without fully approved regulations, completed long-range plans, and basic financial-control disciplines, which then contaminates land development, contracting, allotment, and accountability.
binding_constraint_top3: Absence of approved planning/regulatory architecture and long-range development plans
Institutional governance and internal-control weakness across the authority
Lack of basic financial-accounting and oversight systems undermining operational discipline
slow_variables: Perspective, development, redevelopment, and zonal planning capability
Regulatory approval and institutional governance arrangements
Internal financial controls and annual-accounting discipline
contributing_factors: UPSIDA was managing affairs without approved regulations and did not finalize key plans since its constitution
Land acquisition and land development targets were repeatedly missed
Contracts were awarded without proper document verification or bidder-capacity assessment in multiple cases
Plot allotment processes showed irregular evaluation, excess allotment, and deviations from approved procedure
Annual accounts of UPSIDA were not prepared since inception
good_interventions_ranked: Fix the planning/governance foundation first: approve regulations, complete perspective/development/zonal plans, and create a disciplined planning baseline for all industrial areas.
Restore internal financial and oversight machinery, including current annual accounts, board/legislature reporting, and digitized investment/control registers.
Then tighten transactional execution with stronger contract verification, bidder-capacity checks, allotment eligibility rules, and documented accountability for deviations.
bad_interventions: Treating each contract or allotment irregularity as an isolated issue
More ambitious target-setting without a real planning and control backbone
Digitization theater that does not fix planning, approval, and accounting gaps
early_warning_signals: Key plans or regulations still pending beyond defined deadlines
Annual accounts and required reports remaining unprepared or unlaid
Repeat bid-verification failures, bidder-capacity exceptions, or irregular allotments
Persistent target misses across acquisition, development, allotment, and recoveries
distractors_to_penalize: Only anti-corruption rhetoric without process redesign
UI or portal upgrades without governance fixes
Volume targets as the main response
evidence: The executive summary says audit found lapses in planning, infrastructure development, allotment of plots, and system of internal control.
It says UPSIDA operated without approved regulations and had not finalized perspective/development/redevelopment plans since its constitution.
The summary notes repeated target misses in land acquisition and land development.
It documents contract-award irregularities, allotment-process problems, and inadequate internal financial controls.
It says annual accounts of UPSIDA were not prepared since inception.
